{{Short description|International governing body for association football in Asia}}
{{Use British English|date=June 2026}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=June 2026}}

|title=AFC STATUTES Edition 2017 |publisher=AFC |date=8 May 2017 |access-date=15 January 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171227060248/http://res.cloudinary.com/deltatreafcprod/image/upload/dy0ume51u0qkfsadlp6q.pdf |archive-date=27 December 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

| leader_name         = [[Salman bin Ibrahim Al Khalifa]]

| [[Zaw Zaw]] ([[ASEAN Football Federation|AFF]])

| Hachem Haidar ([[West Asian Football Federation|WAFF]])
}}}}
| leader_title3       = Secretary general
| leader_name3        = [[Windsor John]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.the-afc.com/about-afc/gen-secretary/ |title=AFC GENERAL SECRETARY: DATO' WINDSOR JOHN |access-date=4 February 2019 |publisher=Arabian Football Confederation |archive-date=2 November 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191102032717/https://www.the-afc.com/about-afc/gen-secretary/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.thestar.com.my/metro/scoreboard/2015/12/03/prime-choice-to-lead-fifa-sheikh-salman-is-the-favourite-to-take-over-top-post-of-footballs-governin/ |title=Prime choice to lead FIFA |author=Christopher Raj |work=[[The Star (Malaysia)|The Star]] |date=3 December 2015 |access-date=25 March 2016 |archive-date=25 December 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181225012856/https://www.thestar.com.my/metro/scoreboard/2015/12/03/prime-choice-to-lead-fifa-sheikh-salman-is-the-favourite-to-take-over-top-post-of-footballs-governin/ |url-status=live }}</ref>
| subsidiaries             = {{plainlist}}
* [[ASEAN Football Federation|AFF]] (Southeast Asia)
* [[Central Asian Football Association|CAFA]] (Central Asia)
* [[East Asian Football Federation|EAFF]] (East Asia)
* [[South Asian Football Federation|SAFF]] (South Asia)
* [[West Asian Football Federation|WAFF]] (West Asia)
{{endplainlist}}
| parent_organisation = [[FIFA]]
| website             = {{URL|https://www.the-afc.com/|the-afc.com}}
}}
{{FIFA confederations}}
The '''Asian Football Confederation''' ('''AFC''') is the [[Sports governing body|governing body]] of [[association football]], [[beach soccer]], and [[futsal]] in most countries and territories in [[Asia]].{{Efn|name=Outside Asia}} It is one of the six continental confederations under [[FIFA]] and is responsible for organising international competitions for national teams and clubs within its jurisdiction, including the [[AFC Asian Cup]] and [[AFC Champions League Elite]]. Founded in 1954 and headquartered in [[Kuala Lumpur]], Malaysia, the AFC comprises [[List of men's national association football teams#AFC (Asia)|47 member associations]].

==History==
The idea of an Asian Football Confederation was first brought up during the [[1952 Summer Olympics]] in [[Helsinki]], Finland.<ref name=":0">{{Cite news |date=25 November 1984|title=From humble beginnings...|url=https://eresources.nlb.gov.sg/newspapers/digitised/article/singmonitor19841125-1.2.27.3|access-date=3 June 2026|work=[[The Singapore Monitor]]|pages=18}}</ref> On 8 May 1954, the AFC was officially formed at the [[1954 Asian Games]] in [[Manila]], Philippines.<ref name=":1">{{Cite news |date=18 May 2000 |title=New home for AFC |url=https://eresources.nlb.gov.sg/newspapers/digitised/page/straitstimes20000518-1.1.84 |access-date=3 June 2026 |work=[[The Straits Times]] |pages=84}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=All-Asia football association |url=https://eresources.nlb.gov.sg/newspapers/Digitised/Article/freepress19540508-1.2.111?ST=1&AT=filter&DF=&DT=&AO=true&NPT=&L=&CTA=&NID=&CT=&WC=&YR=&k=philippines+football%26ka%3dphilippines+football&P=2&Display=0&filterS=0&QT=philippines,football&oref=article |access-date=21 November 2020 |work=The Straits Times |date=8 May 1954 |quote=The Asian Games (sic) Football Confederation was formed in Manila yesterday. |archive-date=6 September 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210906195335/https://eresources.nlb.gov.sg/newspapers/Digitised/Article/freepress19540508-1.2.111?ST=1&AT=filter&DF=&DT=&AO=true&NPT=&L=&CTA=&NID=&CT=&WC=&YR=&k=philippines+football%26ka%3Dphilippines+football&P=2&Display=0&filterS=0&QT=philippines,football&oref=article |url-status=live }}</ref> The first president was [[Lo Man-kam]] of Hong Kong, and the first office was based in Hong Kong.<ref name=":1" /> The 13 founding members included [[Afghanistan Football Federation|Afghanistan]], [[Myanmar Football Federation|Burma]], [[Football Association of Hong Kong, China|Hong Kong]], [[All India Football Federation|India]], [[Football Association of Indonesia|Indonesia]], [[Israel Football Association|Israel]], [[Japan Football Association|Japan]], [[Pakistan Football Federation|Pakistan]], the [[Philippine Football Federation|Philippines]], [[Football Association of Singapore|Singapore]], [[Korea Football Association|South Korea]], [[Vietnam Football Federation|South Vietnam]], and [[Chinese Taipei Football Association|Taiwan]].<ref>香港足球總會九十週年紀念特刊 (Hong Kong Football Association 90th Anniversary Booklet) 2004</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=AFC 60th Anniversary: Back to where it all began |url=http://www.the-afc.com/60th-anniversary/afc-60th-anniversary-back-to-where-it-all-began |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180928182644/http://www.the-afc.com/60th-anniversary/afc-60th-anniversary-back-to-where-it-all-began |archive-date=28 September 2018 |publisher=Asian Football Confederation}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=AFC's 63rd anniversary: Seven events that shaped Asian football |url=https://www.the-afc.com/en/more/news/afcs_63rd_anniversary_seven_events_that_shaped_asian_football.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240114170748/https://www.the-afc.com/en/more/news/afcs_63rd_anniversary_seven_events_that_shaped_asian_football.html |archive-date=14 January 2024 |publisher=Asian Football 2000Confederation}}</ref>

Of the AFC's original 13 member associations, only seven entered the [[1956 AFC Asian Cup qualification|qualifying tournament]] for the inaugural [[AFC Asian Cup]] in [[1956 AFC Asian Cup|1956]].<ref name=":0" /> Four teams ultimately qualified for the final tournament, which was held in then-British Hong Kong.<ref name=":0" /> The 1956 tournament was the first edition of the AFC Asian Cup, the world's second-oldest continental national-team football competition.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The remarkable rise of Asia's greatest showpiece |url=http://www.the-afc.com/asiancup/news/the-remarkable-rise-of-asia-s-greatest-showpiece |access-date=24 April 2019 |publisher=Asian Football Confederation |language=en-GB}}</ref>

Arab countries entered the Asian Cup qualifiers for the first time in the [[1972 AFC Asian Cup qualification]],<ref name=":0" /> following [[Israel national football team|Israel]]'s withdrawal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://newspapers.nl.sg/Digitised/Article/straitstimes19700816.2.92.41.aspx|title=Malaysia, Singapore stay in Asian Cup central zone|date=16 August 1970|publisher=[[The Straits Times]]|access-date=7 July 2016|archive-date=13 February 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130213160553/http://newspapers.nl.sg/Digitised/Article/straitstimes19700816.2.92.41.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref> Israel was eventually [[Boycotts of Israel in sports|expelled]] from the AFC in 1974, due to political pressure from Arab and Muslim members that [[Arab–Israeli conflict|refused to play against Israel]].<ref name="JTA2">{{cite web |title=Israel Ousted From Asian Football Confederation|url=https://www.jta.org/archive/israel-ousted-from-asian-football-association|website=JTA|date=16 September 1974|publisher=Jewish Telegraphic Agency|access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref>

In 1958, the office relocated from Hong Kong to Malaysia.<ref name=":1" /> In 2000, the AFC House was inaugurated as the AFC's headquarters; based in [[Bukit Jalil]], [[Kuala Lumpur]], the construction cost [[Malaysian ringgit|RM]] 18 million ([[US$]] 8.18 million).<ref name=":1" />

==Women's football==
The Asian Ladies Football Confederation (ALFC) was the section of AFC that managed [[women's association football]] in Asia. The group was independently founded in April 1968 in a meeting involving [[Taiwan]], [[British Hong Kong]], [[Malaysia]], and [[Singapore]]. In 1986, ALFC merged with AFC.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.asiansportsnet.com/football-soccer/|title=Football (Soccer) − Asian Football Confederation|publisher=Asian Sports Net|access-date=25 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160403024732/http://www.asiansportsnet.com/football-soccer/|archive-date=3 April 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Executive committee==
On 1 February 2023, the AFC's member associations elected the AFC Executive Committee for the term 2023 to 2027. Including the [[List of presidents of the Asian Football Confederation|AFC president]], the AFC has seven members on the [[FIFA Council]].<ref name="AFC Congress 2023">{{Cite web |title=AFC Executive Committee and Asia's FIFA Council members confirmed |url=https://www.the-afc.com/en/about_afc/afc_congress/news/afc_executive_committee_and_asias_fifa_council_members_confirmed.html |access-date=2026-06-03 |website=the-AFC |language=en}}</ref>

{{col-begin}}
{{col-break}}

* {{flagicon|BAN}} [[Mahfuza Akhter Kiron]]

* {{flagicon|MAS}} Hamidin Mohd Amin
* {{flagicon|LAO}} Kanya Keomany
* {{flagicon|PHI}} [[Mariano Araneta]]
* {{flagicon|QAT}} [[Hamad Bin Khalifa Bin Ahmed Al-Thani]]
* {{flagicon|KSA}} [[Yasser Al Misehal]]

'''AFC Executive Committee Members'''
* {{flagicon|KUW}} Abdullah Ahmed Alshaheen Alrabeea
* {{flagicon|UAE}} Abdullah Nasser Aljneibi
* {{flagicon|MDV}} Bassam Adeel Jaleel
* {{flagicon|NEP}} [[Pankaj Bikram Nembang]]
* {{flagicon|AUS}} [[Chris Nikou]]
* {{flagicon|THA}} [[Somyot Poompanmoung]]
* {{flagicon|UZB}} [[Ravshan Irmatov]]
* {{flagicon|HKG}} Eric Fok Kai Shan
* {{flagicon|IND}} [[Shaji Prabhakaran]]
* {{flagicon|SRI}} Anura De Silva
{{col-end}}

==List of AFC presidents==
{{Main list|List of presidents of the Asian Football Confederation}}
[[File:Salman Al-Khalifa.jpg|thumb|upright|[[Salman bin Ibrahim Al Khalifa]] (2014) has been president of the AFC since 2013.]]
{|class="wikitable"
!President!!Nationality!!Term
|-
|[[Lo Man-kam]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Hong Kong|1910}}|| 1954
|-
|[[Kwok Chan]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Hong Kong|1955}}|| 1954–1956
|-
|[[William Louey]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Hong Kong|1955}}|| 1956–1957
|-
|[[Chan Nam-cheong]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Hong Kong|1955}}|| 1957–1958
|-
|[[Tunku Abdul Rahman]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Malaysia}}|| 1958–1976
|-
|[[Kambiz Atabay]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Iran|1964}}|| 1976–1978
|-
|[[Hamzah Abu Samah]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Malaysia}}|| 1978–1994
|-
|[[Ahmad Shah of Pahang|Ahmad Shah]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Malaysia}}|| 1994–2002
|-
|[[Mohammed bin Hammam]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Qatar}}|| 2002–2011
|-
|[[Zhang Jilong]]|| align="left" | {{flag|China}} || 2011–2013 (acting)
|-
|[[Salman bin Ibrahim Al Khalifa]]|| align="left" | {{flag|Bahrain}} || 2013–present
|}

==Members==
[[File:Asean_Football_Federation_countries.PNG|thumb|250x250px|AFC regional federations]]
Due to the geographical size of Asia, the AFC is subdivided into five sub-confederations:
* [[ASEAN Football Federation]] (AFF) – represents countries in [[Southeast Asia]], plus Australia.
* [[Central Asian Football Association]] (CAFA) – represents countries in [[Central Asia]], comprising Afghanistan, Iran and most of [[Central Asia]], except Kazakhstan.
* [[East Asian Football Federation]] (EAFF) – represents nations in [[East Asia]], plus Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands.
* [[South Asian Football Federation]] (SAFF) – represents countries in [[South Asia]].
* [[West Asian Football Federation]] (WAFF) – represents countries in [[West Asia]], except Israel.
{{Clear|right}}

{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center"
! [[List of FIFA country codes|Code]] !! Association !! class="unsortable"|National teams !! Founded !! FIFA<br />affiliation !! AFC<br />affiliation !! Sub-<br />confederation !! [[International Olympic Committee|IOC]]<br />[[National Olympic Committee|member]]
|-
|AFG ||style="text-align:left"| {{fba|AFG}} || {{flatlist|1={{nft links (AFC)|Afghanistan|sublist_style=font-size:90%}}}}
|1933 || 1948 || 1954 || [[Central Asian Football Association|CAFA]] || {{yes}}
|-
|AUS ||style="text-align:left"| {{fba|AUS}}{{efn|Former member of the Oceania Football Confederation (1966–1972, 1978–2006), joined AFC.}} || {{flatlist|1={{nft links (AFC)|Australia|sublist_style=font-size:90%}}}}
|1961 || 1963 || 2006 || [[ASEAN Football Federation|AFF]] || {{yes}}{{efn|name="oc-onoc"}}
|-

|-

|1952 || 1972 || 1969 || [[ASEAN Football Federation|AFF]] || {{yes}}
|-

|1933 || 1954 || 1954 || [[ASEAN Football Federation|AFF]] || {{yes}}

|-
|HKG ||style="text-align:left"| {{fba|HKG}} || {{flatlist|1={{nft links (AFC)|Hong Kong|sublist_style=font-size:90%}}}}
|1914 || 1954 || 1954 || [[East Asian Football Federation|EAFF]] || {{yes}}

|1937 || 1948 || 1954 || [[South Asian Football Federation|SAFF]] || {{yes}}

|1930 || 1952 || 1954 || [[ASEAN Football Federation|AFF]] || {{yes}}

|-

|1916 || 1925 || 1954 || [[ASEAN Football Federation|AFF]] || {{yes}}

|2002 || 2005 || 2002 || [[ASEAN Football Federation|AFF]] || {{yes}}

|TKM ||style="text-align:left"| {{fba|TKM}} || {{flatlist|1={{nft links (AFC)|Turkmenistan|sublist_style=font-size:90%}}}}
|1992 || 1994 || 1993 || [[Central Asian Football Association|CAFA]] || {{yes}}
|-
|UAE ||style="text-align:left"| {{fba|UAE}} || {{flatlist|1={{nft links (AFC)|United Arab Emirates|sublist_style=font-size:90%}}}}
|1971 || 1974 || 1974 || [[West Asian Football Federation|WAFF]] || {{yes}}
|-
|UZB ||style="text-align:left"| {{fba|UZB}} || {{flatlist|1={{nft links (AFC)|Uzbekistan|sublist_style=font-size:90%}}}}
|1946 || 1994 || 1993 || [[Central Asian Football Association|CAFA]] || {{yes}}
|-
|VIE ||style="text-align:left"| {{fba|VIE}} || {{flatlist|1={{nft links (AFC)|Vietnam|sublist_style=font-size:90%}}}}
|1960 || 1952 || 1954 || [[ASEAN Football Federation|AFF]] || {{yes}}
|-
|YEM ||style="text-align:left"| {{fba|YEM}} || {{flatlist|1={{nft links (AFC)|Yemen|sublist_style=font-size:90%}}}}
|1962 || 1980 || 1980 || [[West Asian Football Federation|WAFF]] || {{yes}}
|}

===Former members===
{| class="wikitable sortable"
![[FIFA country code|{{tooltip|Code|FIFA country code}}]]
!Association
!Year
!Note
|-
| style="text-align:center;" |NZL
| style="text-align:left;" |{{fba|NZL}}
|1964<ref>{{cite web |date=28 August 1964 |title=AFC TELLS INDONESIA: PAY OR BE SACKED |url=http://eresources.nlb.gov.sg/newspapers/Digitised/Article/straitstimes19640828-1.2.130.6.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150527023753/http://eresources.nlb.gov.sg/newspapers/Digitised/Article/straitstimes19640828-1.2.130.6.aspx |archive-date=27 May 2015 |access-date=8 December 2014 |publisher=[[The Straits Times]]}}</ref>
|Founding member of the [[Oceania Football Confederation|OFC]] in 1966.
|-
| style="text-align:center;" |ISR
| style="text-align:left;" |{{fba|ISR}}
|1954–1974
|Joined UEFA after international matches against national teams representing [[Arab world|Arab countries]] boycotted by the [[West Asian Football Federation]].<ref>{{cite news |date=16 September 1974 |title=Aust-Asian bid fails |url=https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=RNRYAAAAIBAJ&pg=4321%2C5571494 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210624203417/https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=RNRYAAAAIBAJ&pg=4321,5571494 |archive-date=24 June 2021 |access-date=10 July 2014 |work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |page=11}}</ref><ref name="israel">{{cite news |last=Montague |first=James |date=February 27, 2008 |title=Time is right for Israel to return to its Asian roots |url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2008/feb/27/worldcup2010 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171023230949/https://www.theguardian.com/football/2008/feb/27/worldcup2010 |archive-date=23 October 2017 |access-date=9 May 2022 |work=The Guardian |location=London}}</ref> Later became a full [[UEFA]] member in 1994.
|-
| style="text-align:center;" |VSO
|{{fba|South Vietnam}}
|{{nowrap|1954–1975}}
|[[Reunification Day|Unified]] with North Vietnam as [[Vietnam Football Federation|Vietnam]].
|-
| style="text-align:center;" |YMD
| style="text-align:left;" |{{fba|YMD}}
|{{nowrap|1972–1990}}<ref>{{cite news |url=http://eresources.nlb.gov.sg/newspapers/Digitised/Article/straitstimes19720728-1.2.132.4.aspx |title=Arabia and Yemen are new members |work=[[The Straits Times]] |date=28 July 1972 |access-date=10 May 2020 |archive-date=4 March 2016 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304090349/http://eresources.nlb.gov.sg/newspapers/Digitised/Article/straitstimes19720728-1.2.132.4.aspx |url-status=live }}</ref>
|[[Yemeni unification|Unified]] with North Yemen as [[Yemen Football Association|Yemen]].
|-
| style="text-align:center;" |KAZ
| style="text-align:left;" |{{fba|KAZ}}
|1993–2002
|Joined [[UEFA]] in 2002.<ref>{{cite web |date=26 June 2002 |title=UEFA delegation visit Kazakhstan |url=https://www.uefa.com/news-media/news/0254-0d7b28553481-d9179376af81-1000--uefa-delegation-visit-kazakhstan/ |publisher=[[UEFA]]}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |date=21 October 2022 |title=The curious case of Kazakh football |url=https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/articles/the-curious-case-of-kazakh-football |publisher=[[FIFA]]}}</ref>
|-
|}

==Competitions==
{{See also|List of association football competitions|FIFA International Match Calendar}}

===AFC competitions===
The AFC organises the [[AFC Asian Cup]] and the [[AFC Women's Asian Cup]], both held every four years. It also runs the [[AFC Futsal Asian Cup]], [[AFC Women's Futsal Asian Cup]], and the [[AFC Beach Soccer Asian Cup]], along with various youth and age-group international association football and futsal tournaments. In addition, the AFC oversees the [[FIFA World Cup qualifiers|Asian qualifying tournaments]] for the [[FIFA World Cup]] and for [[football at the Summer Olympics]].

The premier men's club competition is the [[AFC Champions League Elite]], which is an amalgamation of the [[Asian Club Championship]], the [[Asian Cup Winners' Cup]], and the [[Asian Super Cup]]. It features leading clubs from AFC member associations, with the number of participating teams determined by the ranking of each association, allowing for promotion or relegation of slots based on performance. The top-tier women's club competition is the [[AFC Women's Champions League]], introduced in the [[2024–25 AFC Women's Champions League|2024–25 season]].<ref>[https://www.the-afc.com/en/club/afc_womens_club_championship.html AFC Women's Club Championship] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220215104022/https://www.the-afc.com/en/club/afc_womens_club_championship.html |date=15 February 2022 }} the-afc.com</ref>

The second-tier men's club competition is the [[AFC Champions League Two]], while the third tier is the [[AFC Challenge League]], both of which were restructured and rebranded in 2024.<ref>{{cite web |last=Griffiths |first=Ian |url=http://www.footballasia.com/en/news/index.asp?id=40247&sec=90&ssec=260&mth=7&yr=2005 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20051119163847/http://www.footballasia.com/en/news/index.asp?id=40247&sec=90&ssec=260&mth=7&yr=2005 |archive-date=19 November 2005 |title=Clubs sandwiched |work=FootballAsia.com |access-date=19 July 2005}}</ref>

The AFC also organises the annual [[AFC Futsal Club Championship]], the continent’s premier club futsal competition.<ref>[https://www.the-afc.com/en/club/afc_futsal_club_championship.html AFC Futsal Club Championship] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220122025634/https://www.the-afc.com/en/club/afc_futsal_club_championship.html |date=22 January 2022 }} the-afc.com</ref>{{col-begin}}
{{col-break}}
'''National teams:'''
;Men
*[[AFC Asian Cup]]
*[[AFC Nations League]]
*[[AFC U-23 Asian Cup]]
*[[AFC U-20 Asian Cup]]
*[[AFC U-17 Asian Cup]]
*[[AFC Futsal Asian Cup]]
*[[AFC U-20 Futsal Asian Cup]]
*[[AFC Beach Soccer Asian Cup]]
;Women

*[[AFC U-20 Women's Asian Cup]]
*[[AFC U-17 Women's Asian Cup]]
*[[AFC Women's Olympic Qualifying Tournament]]
*[[AFC Women's Futsal Asian Cup]]
;Esports
*[[AFC eAsian Cup]]
{{col-break}}
'''Clubs:'''
;Men
*[[AFC Champions League Elite]]
*[[AFC Champions League Two]]
*[[AFC Challenge League]]
*[[AFC Futsal Club Championship]]
;Women

'''National teams:'''
;Men
*[[AFC Solidarity Cup]]
*[[AFC Challenge Cup]]
*[[Afro-Asian Cup of Nations]]
*[[AFC–OFC Challenge Cup]]
*[[AFC U-14 Championship]]<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.the-afc.com/competitions/afc-u-14-championship/latest/news/iraq-crowned-afc-u-14-champions--8269| title = the-afc.com| access-date = 13 September 2021| archive-date = 13 September 2021| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20210913015042/https://www.the-afc.com/competitions/afc-u-14-championship/latest/news/iraq-crowned-afc-u-14-champions--8269| url-status = live}}</ref>
;Women
*[[AFC U-14 Girls' Regional Championship]]<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.the-afc.com/competitions/afc-u-14-girls-regional-championship/latest/news/curtains-down-on-u-14-girls-regionals-first-edition-5417| title = the-afc.com| access-date = 13 September 2021| archive-date = 13 September 2021| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20210913015047/https://www.the-afc.com/competitions/afc-u-14-girls-regional-championship/latest/news/curtains-down-on-u-14-girls-regionals-first-edition-5417| url-status = live}}</ref>
'''Clubs:'''
;Men
*[[Asian Cup Winners' Cup]]
*[[Asian Super Cup]]
*[[Afro-Asian Club Championship]]
*[[AFC Futsal Club Championship]]
;Women
*[[AFC Women's Club Championship]]
{{col-end}}

==FIFA World Rankings==
{{See also|FIFA Men's World Rankings|FIFA Women's World Rankings}}

===Overview===
{{col-begin}}
{{col-break}}
{{Sports rankings table|FIFA World Rankings|1|211|select=AFC|style=
|caption=FIFA Men's Rankings (as of INSERT_UPDATE_DATE)
|footer1= *Local rankings based on FIFA ranking points
|selection_header = AFC*
|rank_header=FIFA
|change_header=+/-
|team_header=National Team
|points_header=Points
}}
{{col-break}}
{{Sports rankings table|FIFA Women's World Rankings|1|211|select=AFC|style=
|caption=FIFA Women's Rankings (as of INSERT_UPDATE_DATE)
|footer1= *Local rankings based on FIFA ranking points
|selection_header = AFC*
|rank_header=FIFA
|change_header=+/-
|team_header=National Team
|points_header=Points
}}
{{col-end}}

==Major tournament records==
;Legend
*{{bg|gold|&nbsp;1st&nbsp;}} – Champions
*{{bg|silver|&nbsp;2nd&nbsp;}} – Runners-up
*{{bg|#CC9966|&nbsp;3rd&nbsp;}} – Third place
*{{bg|#9ACDFF|&nbsp;4th&nbsp;}} – Fourth place
*{{bg|#81c846|&nbsp;SF&nbsp;}} – Semi-finals
*QF – Quarter-finals (1934–1938, 1954–1970, and 1986–present)
*R3 — Round 3 (2026–present: knockout round of 16)
*R2 — Round 2 (1974–1978: second group stage, top 8; 1982: second group stage, top 12; 1986–2022: knockout round of 16; 2026–present: knockout round of 32)
*R1 — Round 1 (1930, 1950–1970 and 1986–present: group stage; 1934–1938: knockout round of 16; 1974–1982: first group stage)
*{{border|width=1px|color=gray|&nbsp;''Q''&nbsp;}} — Qualified for upcoming tournament
*{{border|width=1px|color=gray|&nbsp;'''••'''&nbsp;}} — Qualified but withdrew
*{{border|width=1px|color=gray|&nbsp;'''•'''&nbsp;}} — Did not qualify
*{{border|width=1px|color=gray|&nbsp;×&nbsp;}} — Did not enter / withdrawn / banned / disqualified
*{{border|width=3px|color=red|&nbsp; &nbsp;}}&nbsp;— Hosts

For each tournament, the flag of the host country and the number of teams in each finals tournament (in brackets) are shown.

===FIFA World Cup===
{{Main|National team appearances in the FIFA World Cup}}
{{See also|Asian nations at the FIFA World Cup}}
<div style="overflow-x:auto;">
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; font-size:85%;"
!colspan=39|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
|-
! Team
![[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]]<br />{{Flagicon|Uruguay}}<br />(13)
![[1934 FIFA World Cup|1934]]<br />{{Flagicon|Italy|1861}}<br />(16)
![[1938 FIFA World Cup|1938]]<br />{{Flagicon|France}}<br />(15)
![[1950 FIFA World Cup|1950]]<br />{{Flagicon|Brazil|1889}}<br />(13)
![[1954 FIFA World Cup|1954]]<br />{{Flagicon|Switzerland}}<br />(16)
![[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]<br />{{Flagicon|Sweden}}<br />(16)
![[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]<br />{{Flagicon|Chile}}<br />(16)
![[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]<br />{{Flagicon|England}}<br />(16)
![[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]<br />{{Flagicon|Mexico}}<br />(16)
![[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]<br />{{Flagicon|West Germany}}<br />(16)
![[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]<br />{{Flagicon|Argentina}}<br />(16)
![[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]<br />{{Flagicon|Spain}}<br />(24)
![[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]<br />{{Flagicon|Mexico}}<br />(24)
![[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]<br />{{Flagicon|Italy}}<br />(24)
![[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]<br />{{Flagicon|United States of America}}<br />(24)
![[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]<br />{{Flagicon|France|1974}}<br />(32)
![[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]<br />{{Flagicon|Japan}}<br />{{Flagicon|South Korea|1997}}<br />(32)
![[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]<br />{{Flagicon|Germany}}<br />(32)
![[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]<br />{{Flagicon|South Africa}}<br />(32)
![[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]<br />{{Flagicon|Brazil}}<br />(32)
![[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]<br />{{Flagicon|Russia}}<br />(32)
![[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]<br />{{Flagicon|Qatar}}<br />(32)
![[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]<br />{{Flagicon|Canada}}<br />{{Flagicon|Mexico}}<br />{{Flagicon|United States of America}}<br />(48)
![[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]<br />{{Flagicon|Morocco}}<br />{{Flagicon|Portugal}}<br />{{Flagicon|Spain}}<br />(48)
![[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]<br />{{Flagicon|Saudi Arabia}}<br />(48)

===AFC Asian Cup===
{{main|AFC Asian Cup records and statistics}}
<div style="overflow-x:auto;">
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center; font-size:90%;"
!colspan=39|[[AFC Asian Cup]] record
|-
! Team<br />(Total 35 teams)
! [[1956 AFC Asian Cup|1956]]<br />{{flagicon|Hong Kong|1955}}<br />(4)
! [[1960 AFC Asian Cup|1960]]<br />{{flagicon|KOR|1949}}<br />(4)
! [[1964 AFC Asian Cup|1964]]<br />{{flagicon|Israel}}<br />(4)
! [[1968 AFC Asian Cup|1968]]<br />{{flagicon|Iran|1964}}<br />(5)
! [[1972 AFC Asian Cup|1972]]<br />{{flagicon|Thailand}}<br />(6)
! [[1976 AFC Asian Cup|1976]]<br />{{flagicon|Iran|1964}}<br />(6)
! [[1980 AFC Asian Cup|1980]]<br />{{flagicon|Kuwait}}<br />(10)
! [[1984 AFC Asian Cup|1984]]<br />{{flagicon|Singapore}}<br />(10)
! [[1988 AFC Asian Cup|1988]]<br />{{flagicon|Qatar}}<br />(10)
! [[1992 AFC Asian Cup|1992]]<br />{{flagicon|Japan|1870}}<br />(8)
! [[1996 AFC Asian Cup|1996]]<br />{{flagicon|United Arab Emirates}}<br />(12)
! [[2000 AFC Asian Cup|2000]]<br />{{flagicon|Lebanon}}<br />(12)
! [[2004 AFC Asian Cup|2004]]<br />{{flagicon|China}}<br />(16)
! [[2007 AFC Asian Cup|2007]]<br />{{flagicon|Indonesia}}<br />{{flagicon|Malaysia}}<br />{{flagicon|Thailand}}<br />{{flagicon|Vietnam}}<br />(16)
! [[2011 AFC Asian Cup|2011]]<br />{{flagicon|Qatar}}<br />(16)
! [[2015 AFC Asian Cup|2015]]<br />{{flagicon|Australia}}<br />(16)
! [[2019 AFC Asian Cup|2019]]<br />{{flagicon|United Arab Emirates}}<br />(24)
! [[2023 AFC Asian Cup|2023]]<br />{{flagicon|Qatar}}<br />(24)
! [[2027 AFC Asian Cup|2027]]<br />{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}}<br />(24)
! Years

|-
! Team !! [[1991 FIFA Women's World Cup|1991]]<br />{{Flag icon|China}}<br />(12) !! [[1995 FIFA Women's World Cup|1995]]<br />{{Flag icon|Sweden}}<br />(12) !! [[1999 FIFA Women's World Cup|1999]]<br />{{Flag icon|USA}}<br />(16) !! [[2003 FIFA Women's World Cup|2003]]<br />{{Flag icon|USA}}<br />(16) !! [[2007 FIFA Women's World Cup|2007]]<br />{{Flag icon|China}}<br />(16) !! [[2011 FIFA Women's World Cup|2011]]<br />{{Flag icon|Germany}}<br />(16) !! [[2015 FIFA Women's World Cup|2015]] <br />{{Flag icon|CAN}}<br />(24)!! [[2019 FIFA Women's World Cup|2019]]<br />{{Flag icon|FRA}}<br />(24) !! [[2023 FIFA Women's World Cup|2023]]<br />{{flagicon|Australia}} {{flagicon|New Zealand}}<br />(32) !! [[2027 FIFA Women's World Cup|2027]]<br />{{Flag icon|BRA}}<br />(32) !! Years

===Olympic Games===
====Men's tournament====
{{See also|Football at the Summer Olympics#Participating nations}}

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; font-size:80%;"
! colspan="32" |[[Football at the Summer Olympics|Olympic Games]] ([[Football at the Summer Olympics#Men's tournament|Men's tournament]]) record
|-
! Team|| [[Football at the 1900 Summer Olympics|1900]]<br />{{flagicon|France}}<br />(3) || colspan="2"| [[Football at the 1904 Summer Olympics|1904]]<br />{{flagicon|United States}}<br />(3) || [[Football at the 1908 Summer Olympics|1908]]<br />{{flagicon|Great Britain}}<br />(6) || [[Football at the 1912 Summer Olympics|1912]]<br />{{flagicon|Sweden}}<br />(11) || [[Football at the 1920 Summer Olympics|1920]]<br />{{flagicon|Belgium}}<br />(14) || [[Football at the 1924 Summer Olympics|1924]]<br />{{flagicon|France}}<br />(22) || [[Football at the 1928 Summer Olympics|1928]]<br />{{flagicon|Netherlands}}<br />(17) || [[Football at the 1936 Summer Olympics|1936]]<br />{{flagicon|Germany|Nazi}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 1948 Summer Olympics|1948]]<br />{{flagicon|United Kingdom}}<br />(18) || [[Football at the 1952 Summer Olympics|1952]]<br />{{flagicon|Finland}}<br />(25) || [[Association football at the 1956 Summer Olympics|1956]]<br />{{flagicon|Australia}}<br />(11) || [[Football at the 1960 Summer Olympics|1960]]<br />{{flagicon|Italy}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 1964 Summer Olympics|1964]]<br />{{flagicon|Japan|1947}}<br />(14) || [[Football at the 1968 Summer Olympics|1968]]<br />{{flagicon|Mexico}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 1972 Summer Olympics|1972]]<br />{{flagicon|FRG}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 1976 Summer Olympics|1976]]<br />{{flagicon|Canada}}<br />(13) || [[Football at the 1980 Summer Olympics|1980]]<br />{{flagicon|Soviet Union|1955}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 1984 Summer Olympics|1984]]<br />{{flagicon|United States}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 1988 Summer Olympics|1988]]<br />{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 1992 Summer Olympics|1992]]<br />{{flagicon|Spain}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 1996 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|1996]]<br />{{flagicon|United States}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 2000 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2000]]<br />{{flagicon|Australia}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 2004 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2004]]<br />{{flagicon|Greece}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 2008 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2008]]<br />{{flagicon|China}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 2012 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2012]]<br />{{flagicon|GBR}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2016]]<br />{{flagicon|Brazil}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 2020 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2020]]<br />{{flagicon|Japan}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 2024 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2024]]<br />{{flagicon|France}}<br />(16) || [[Football at the 2028 Summer Olympics – Men's tournament|2028]]<br />{{flagicon|USA}}<br />(12) || Years

===AFC Women's Asian Cup===
{{See also|AFC Women's Asian Cup#Comprehensive team results by tournament}}
<div style="overflow-x:auto;">
{|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; font-size:90%"
! colspan="24" |[[AFC Women's Asian Cup]] record
|-
! Team<br />(Total 23 teams)
! [[1975 AFC Women's Championship|1975]]<br />{{flagicon|HKG|1959}}<br />(6)
! [[1977 AFC Women's Championship|1977]]<br />{{flagicon|Republic of China}}<br />(6)
! [[1979 AFC Women's Championship|1979]]<br />{{flagicon|IND}}<br />(6)
! [[1981 AFC Women's Championship|1981]]<br />{{flagicon|HKG|1959}}<br />(8)
! [[1983 AFC Women's Championship|1983]]<br />{{flagicon|THA}}<br />(6)
! [[1986 AFC Women's Championship|1986]]<br />{{flagicon|HKG|1959}}<br />(7)
! [[1989 AFC Women's Championship|1989]]<br />{{flagicon|HKG|1959}}<br />(8)
! [[1991 AFC Women's Championship|1991]]<br />{{flagicon|JPN|1947}}<br />(9)
! [[1993 AFC Women's Championship|1993]]<br />{{flagicon|MAS}}<br />(8)
! [[1995 AFC Women's Championship|1995]]<br />{{flagicon|MAS}}<br />(11)
! [[1997 AFC Women's Championship|1997]]<br />{{flagicon|CHN}}<br />(11)

|
| style="border: 3px solid red" | WD{{efn|India failed to name the required 13 players and were unable to play their match
of the group stage against Chinese Taipei due to them having fewer than 13 players left with the remaining team members testing positive for [[COVID-19]]. They were considered to have withdrawn from the tournament, and all previous matches played by them were considered "null and void" and would not be considered in determining the final group rankings.<ref name="India">{{cite web|url=https://www.the-afc.com/en/national/afc_womens_asian_cup/news/latest_update_on_the_afc_women%E2%80%99s_asian_cup_india_2022%E2%84%A2.html|title=Latest update on the AFC Women's Asian Cup India 2022|publisher=Asian Football Confederation|date=23 January 2022|access-date=14 December 2022|archive-date=23 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220123142325/https://www.the-afc.com/en/national/afc_womens_asian_cup/news/latest_update_on_the_afc_women%E2%80%99s_asian_cup_india_2022%E2%84%A2.html|url-status=live}}</ref>}}
| GS
|

![[1995 FIFA World Youth Championship|1995]]<br />{{Flagicon|Qatar}}<br />(16)

===FIFA U-17 World Cup===
{{main|FIFA U-17 World Cup records and statistics}}
<div style="overflow-x:auto;">

===FIFA Futsal World Cup===
{{main|FIFA Futsal World Cup#Comprehensive team results by tournament}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; font-size:90%"
!colspan=12|[[FIFA Futsal World Cup]] record
|-
! width=170|Team
! {{Flagicon|Netherlands}}<br />[[1989 FIFA Futsal World Championship|1989]]<br />(16) !! style="border:3px solid red"| {{Flagicon|Hong Kong|1959}}<br />[[1992 FIFA Futsal World Championship|1992]]<br />(16) !! {{Flagicon|Spain}}<br />[[1996 FIFA Futsal World Championship|1996]]<br />(16) !! {{Flagicon|Guatemala}}<br />[[2000 FIFA Futsal World Championship|2000]]<br />(16) !! style="border:3px solid red"| {{Flagicon|Chinese Taipei}}<br />[[2004 FIFA Futsal World Championship|2004]]<br />(16) !! {{Flagicon|Brazil}}<br />[[2008 FIFA Futsal World Cup|2008]]<br />(20) !! style="border:3px solid red"| {{Flagicon|Thailand}}<br />[[2012 FIFA Futsal World Cup|2012]]<br />(24) !! {{Flagicon|Colombia}}<br />[[2016 FIFA Futsal World Cup|2016]]<br />(24) !! {{Flagicon|LIT}}<br />[[2021 FIFA Futsal World Cup|2021]]<br />(24) !! style="border:3px solid red"| {{Flagicon|Uzbekistan}}<br />[[2024 FIFA Futsal World Cup|2024]]<br />(24)!!
